title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 01
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.stem: The world's largest gold mine located in:
questions.solution: Grasberg, Indonesia

questions.solution: BCCL

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills twice as fast as B. Both together fill in 20 min. A alone takes:
questions.solution: 30 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Excluding stops, train speed is 72 km/h; including stops, 60 km/h. Stopping time per hour:
questions.solution: 10 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a trapezium, parallel sides are 20 and 30 cm, distance between them 10 cm. Area:
questions.solution: 250 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Four cards are drawn from 52. Probability all of same suit:
questions.solution: 4 × ¹³C₄ / ⁵²C₄

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average of squares of first 10 natural numbers is:

questions.stem: A solution of acid:water = 3:5. 16 L replaced with water gives 1:3. Original volume:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Find the greatest number that divides 398, 536, and 698 leaving remainders 7, 11, and 13 respectively:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In how many ways can 3 prizes be given to 10 students if each gets at most one?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The smallest 5-digit number exactly divisible by 39 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a mixture of 60 L, milk:water = 2:1. How much water must be added to make ratio 1:2?

questions.stem: A is 2 years older than B and B is 3 years older than C. Sum of ages is 55. Age of C:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Probability that a leap year has 53 Sundays:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man spends 40% on food, 25% on rent, 15% on children. If savings are ₹8,000, his income is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A right circular cone has slant height 25 cm and radius 7 cm. Total surface area:
questions.solution: 704 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In an exam, 38% failed in Maths, 42% failed in English, 20% failed in both. Percentage passed:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat downstream 48 km in 4 h, upstream 36 km in 6 h. Speed in still water:
questions.solution: 9 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A product is sold at 25% profit. If cost rises 20% and selling price rises 10%, new profit percent:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a number is first increased by 10% and then decreased by 10%, net change is:
questions.solution: 1% decrease

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: AD, GJ, MP, ?
questions.solution: SV

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which word remains the same in a mirror (vertical axis)?
questions.solution: MOM

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All flowers are plants. Some plants are trees. No tree is bush. Conclusions: I. Some flowers are trees. II. Some plants are not bushes.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Two positions of a dice show 1 opposite 6, 2 opposite 5, 3 opposite 4. Which number is opposite 6?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong number: 10, 14, 28, 32, 64, 68, 136

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: FLP, HNR, JPT, ?
questions.solution: LRV

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Mirror image of 'CALCULATOR' placed horizontally:
questions.solution: ROTALUCLAC

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Sequence of curved lines: ), ), ), ), ? (each is a pair)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. No animal is bird. Conclusions: I. Some cats are not birds. II. Some animals are cats.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A 6×6×6 cube painted red, green, blue on opposite faces. Number of 1×1 cubes with no paint:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: AB, EF, IJ, MN, ?
questions.solution: QR

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If mirror is placed to the right of the word EXAM, the image shows:
questions.solution: MAXE

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Shapes: Square (4 sides), Pentagon (5), Hexagon (6), ?
questions.solution: Heptagon (7)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No A is B. Some B are C. All C are D. Conclusions: I. Some D are not A. II. No A is C.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A dice numbered 1-6. If 1 is at top and 2 at front, which is at bottom if standard dice?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Triangle : 3 :: Pentagon : ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: From home, school is 6 km west. Market is 8 km south of school. Hospital is 6 km east of market. Hospital from home:
questions.solution: 8 km south

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 'P + Q' means P is father of Q, 'P - Q' means P is mother of Q, 'P × Q' means P is brother of Q, 'P ÷ Q' means P is sister of Q, then in 'A ÷ B + C - D', how is A related to D?
questions.solution: Grandmother

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A table shows 5 products with cost and selling price. Product with highest profit %:
questions.solution: Calculated from data

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If STONE = 88, SAND = 45, GRAVEL = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Six kids sit in circle. P is opposite Q. R is between P and S. T is left of Q. U is right of P. Who is between Q and U?
questions.solution: T

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is 5 km south of B. C is 12 km east of A. D is 5 km north of C. Distance B to D:
questions.solution: 12 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 4, 6, 12, 30, 84, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is capable ____ doing the job independently.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To grease the wheels':
questions.solution: To facilitate smooth progress

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager asked ____ the report was ready.
questions.solution: whether

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To play devil's advocate':
options: To argue for the opposite side
questions.solution: To argue for the opposite side

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ a nice person that everyone likes him.
questions.solution: such

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To hit the nail on the head':
questions.solution: To be exactly right

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She speaks ____ softly that no one can hear her.
questions.solution: so

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be caught between two stools':
questions.solution: To be unable to choose

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Hardly had we left ____ it started raining.
questions.solution: when

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To get down to brass tacks':
questions.solution: To focus on essentials

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee consists ____ ten members.
questions.solution: of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To be under the weather':
questions.solution: To feel ill

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company is committed ____ improving safety.
questions.solution: to

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To make no bones about':
questions.solution: To speak frankly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He was prevented ____ attending the meeting.
questions.solution: from

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Meaning of 'To beat around the bush':
questions.solution: To avoid the main topic

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was completed ____ schedule.
questions.solution: ahead of

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: monitoring / technology / improved / has / safety / mine
options: Improved technology has mine safety monitoring
Monitoring mine safety has technology improved
Technology has improved mine safety monitoring
Mine safety monitoring technology has improved
questions.solution: Technology has improved mine safety monitoring

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The data is being analysed.
options: The data has been being analysed.
questions.solution: The data are being analysed.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The professor (A) / gave a (B) / one-hour (C) / lecture. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'one hour'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The informations (A) / provided in (B) / the report (C) / is accurate. (D)
questions.solution: A - should be 'information'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of TRANQUIL:
questions.solution: Serene

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I would rather (A) / prefer tea (B) / than coffee (C) / in the morning. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'have tea'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of JUBILANT:
questions.solution: Joyful

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of MUNDANE:
questions.solution: Ordinary

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boudinage:
options: Boudin (barrel-shaped) structure in competent layer stretched in incompetent matrix
questions.solution: Boudinage: competent layer (quartzite) boudins in incompetent matrix (schist). Necking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mantle plume hypothesis:
options: Column of hot material rising from deep mantle (CMB). Drives hotspot volcanism
questions.solution: Plume: deep origin (D″ layer). Hawaiian chain. Iceland. Reunion. Plume head → flood basalt.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: X-ray diffraction identifies minerals by:
options: Unique crystal structure producing characteristic diffraction pattern
questions.solution: XRD: Bragg law. Each mineral has unique d-spacings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metallogenic provinces in India:
questions.solution: Singhbhum: Cu-U-Fe (Precambrian). Khetri: Cu (Proterozoic). Kolar: Au (Archean greenstone).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Radiogenic isotope 87Rb decays to:
questions.solution: Rb-Sr dating: 87Rb → 87Sr. Half-life ~48.8 Ga. Used for igneous and metamorphic rocks. Isochron method.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic fluid: H₂O ± CO₂. In pelites, fluid is mainly:
options: H₂O (released from breakdown of hydrous minerals)
questions.solution: Metamorphic fluid: H₂O from muscovite, chlorite breakdown. CO₂ in marbles, calc-silicates.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Garnet porphyroblast with inclusion trail (Si/Se) indicates:
options: Timing of growth relative to deformation
questions.solution: Inclusion trails: show garnet grew during folding. Rotational vs non-rotational help determine syn-kinematic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Law of faunal succession: fossils succeed each other in definite order.
options: Same fossil assemblage never recurs in different ages
questions.solution: William Smith: each formation has distinctive fossils. Index fossils for correlation.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Desert pavement is formed by:
options: Deflation removing fines, leaving lag gravel
questions.solution: Desert pavement: surface of closely packed stones. Wind removes fine particles (deflation), leaving coarse lag.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geophysical method — MASW:
options: Multichannel analysis of surface waves. Shear wave velocity (Vs30)
questions.solution: MASW: dispersion curve inversion. Vs30 for seismic site classification (NEHRP).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tension gashes (en échelon):
options: Arrays of quartz/calcite veins. Sigmoidal shape = shear. Angle to shear direction → sense
questions.solution: Tension gashes: opening perpendicular to σ₃. Rotated with progressive shear.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Periglacial landforms:
options: Patterned ground (sorted circles, polygons), pingos, ice wedges, solifluction lobes
questions.solution: Periglacial: freeze-thaw cycles. Permafrost (continuous, discontinuous). Pingos: ice-cored mound.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite classification — S, I, A, M types:
options: S (sedimentary source, peraluminous), I (igneous source, metaluminous), A (anorogenic, alkaline), M (mantle)
questions.solution: S-type: muscovite, monazite. I-type: hornblende, titanite. A-type: fluorite, high Ga/Al.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Transmissivity equals:
options: Hydraulic conductivity multiplied by aquifer thickness
questions.solution: T = Kb. Units: m2/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Polymorph: same composition, different crystal structure:
options: Carbon: diamond (cubic) vs graphite (hexagonal). SiO₂: quartz vs coesite vs stishovite
questions.solution: Polymorph: diamond/graphite. Calcite/aragonite. Rutile/anatase/brookite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold — chevron vs concentric:
options: Chevron: straight limbs, sharp hinge. Concentric: constant layer thickness (parallel)
questions.solution: Chevron: similar folds. Concentric: parallel folds. Detachment folds above décollement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic wave velocities in Earth's layers:
options: Crust: 6-7 km/s. Upper mantle: 8 km/s. Lower mantle: 13 km/s. Outer core: 8 km/s. Inner core: 11 km/s
questions.solution: P-wave velocity generally increases with depth. Drop at CMB (liquid outer core).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Birefringence is:
questions.solution: Birefringence: n_epsilon - n_omega. Measured on universal stage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 3 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 5.73 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 3×5.73 = 17.19.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Coastal erosion management: groynes work by:
questions.solution: Groynes: perpendicular structures trap sediment. Protect updrift side. Can cause downdrift erosion. Part of coastal defense.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 6 km/s and arrives 10 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 6×10 = 60 km.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Tidal rhythmites record:
questions.solution: Rhythmites: alternating thick-thin laminae. Record spring (thick) and neap (thin) tides. Used to calculate Earth-Moon distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mud cracks:
options: Desiccation cracks in wet sediment. Polygonal. Top of bed indicator
questions.solution: Mud cracks: form by drying. Filled by overlying sediment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 25 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal form: pinacoid (basal pinacoid):
questions.solution: Pinacoid: {001} — two parallel faces. Closed form when combined with other forms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity survey: Bouguer anomaly correction for terrain:
options: Accounts for gravitational effect of topography around station
questions.solution: Terrain: mass correction for hills/valleys. Spherical cap or prism integration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laccolith:
options: Concordant intrusion that domes overlying strata
questions.solution: Laccolith: mushroom-shaped. Overlying beds arched upward.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of orthoclase feldspar:
questions.solution: Orthoclase (KAlSi3O8) — hardness 6. Two good cleavages at 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement is INCORRECT?
options: Granite cools faster than basalt, giving it finer grains
questions.solution: Granite cools slowly at depth (coarse grains); basalt cools fast at the surface (fine grains) — the claim is reversed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Molluscan shell mineralogy:
options: Aragonite (most), calcite (some), both (some). Not silica (except in diatoms)
questions.solution: Molluscs: aragonite common. Bivalves: aragonite or calcite. Gastropods: aragonite. Cephalopods: aragonite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Blueschist facies: key mineral glaucophane:
options: Blue Na-amphibole. Stable at high P, low T (subduction)
questions.solution: Glaucophane: Na₂(Mg₃Al₂)Si₈O₂₂(OH)₂. Blue color. Subduction zone metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pumice:
options: Highly vesicular felsic volcanic glass (floats on water)
questions.solution: Pumice: abundant gas bubbles. Used as lightweight aggregate, abrasive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slope stability — factor of safety:
questions.solution: FS = (c·L + W·cosθ·tanφ)/(W·sinθ). Limit equilibrium methods (Bishop, Janbu).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Satellite sensor spatial resolution: Landsat vs Sentinel-2 vs high-res (WorldView):
questions.solution: Landsat: 30m (multispectral), 15m (pan). Sentinel-2: 10m. WorldView: 0.31m (pan), 1.24m (MS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NDVI range: -1 to +1. Water bodies typically have NDVI:
questions.solution: Water: low NIR reflectance, negative NDVI. Vegetation: 0.6-0.9. Bare soil: 0.1-0.2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrological modeling in GIS:
options: Flow accumulation, stream network, watershed delineation. Sink filling required
questions.solution: DEM → fill sinks → flow direction (D8) → flow accumulation → stream threshold → watershed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Web GIS: WMS (Web Map Service) vs WFS (Web Feature Service):
options: WMS: rendered images. WFS: vector features (editable)
questions.solution: WMS: map images (PNG). WFS: GML/GeoJSON features. WCS: coverage (raster data).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Microwave remote sensing — radar:
options: Active sensor. Day/night, cloud penetration. SAR: synthetic aperture
questions.solution: Radar: transmits pulses. Backscatter depends on roughness, moisture, geometry. Interferometry.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological considerations for dam sites:
options: Foundation on competent rock, watertight abutments, no active faults
questions.solution: Dam: avoid karst (leakage), shears, soluble rocks. Grout curtain for seepage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nepheline syenite:
options: Felsic plutonic with nepheline instead of quartz. Silica-undersaturated
questions.solution: Nepheline syenite: nepheline replaces quartz. No free SiO2. Alkaline suite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the undisturbed stratigraphic column shown (top to base: A, B, C, D), the oldest layer is:
questions.solution: By the law of superposition, the lowest bed (D) is the oldest in an undisturbed sequence.

figure.caption: Stratigraphic column

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Beach and shoreface facies:
options: Well-sorted, rounded, laminated sand. Swash and trough cross-bedding
questions.solution: Beach: high energy. Heavy mineral placers (ilmenite, garnet, magnetite).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Index minerals (Barrovian):
questions.solution: Index minerals indicate metamorphic grade. Each stable in specific P-T window.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR): frequency vs depth:
options: Higher frequency (500MHz): higher resolution, shallower. Lower (50MHz): deeper, lower resolution
questions.solution: GPR: 10-2000 MHz. Depth: 1m (500MHz) to 50m (25MHz). Resolution: λ/4.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Placer deposits:
options: Heavy mineral concentration by water/wind. Gold, tin, diamond, ilmenite, zircon
questions.solution: Placer: alluvial, beach, eluvial, paleoplacer. Black sand: ilmenite + magnetite. Witwatersrand gold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL):
options: Quartz/feldspar. Last exposure to sunlight → burial age. Range ~100-200ka
questions.solution: OSL: quartz OSL (blue light stimulation). IRSL: feldspar (IR stimulation). SAR protocol.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Von Mises stress criterion:
questions.solution: Von Mises: equivalent stress σ_eq = √((σ₁-σ₂)²+(σ₂-σ₃)²+(σ₃-σ₁)²)/√2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 15 m³ of coal, 30 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/15 = 2 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of corundum on Mohs scale:
questions.solution: Corundum (Al2O3) — 9. Sapphire and ruby are gem varieties.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Playa lake:
options: Temporary lake in arid basin. Evaporite deposits. Salt crust
questions.solution: Playa: sabkha (coastal). Ephemeral. Evaporite minerals: halite, gypsum, borax.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Normalized Difference Water Index (NDWI):
questions.solution: NDWI: McFeeters (1996). Positive values = water. MNDWI: uses SWIR instead of NIR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological hazards in dams:
questions.solution: RIS: impoundment triggers small quakes. Koyna (M6.3, 1967). Induced seismicity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boninite: high-Mg, high-Si volcanic rock. Tectonic setting:
questions.solution: Boninite: MgO>8%, SiO₂>52%, TiO₂<0.5%. Forearc setting. High-T partial melting of hydrated mantle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Indian Stratigraphy: Vindhyan Supergroup age:
options: Proterozoic (~1.6-0.6 Ga). Older and younger Vindhyan
questions.solution: Vindhyan: Semri, Kaimur, Rewa, Bhander groups. ~1.6 Ga to ~0.6 Ga.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Breccia:
questions.solution: Breccia: angular fragments. Near-source deposition (fault zones, talus).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eclogite to granulite transition (decompression):
questions.solution: Decompression: omphacite → low-Na Cpx + Pl (symplectite). Garnet → Opx + Pl + Sp.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 8 km/s and arrives 30 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 8×30 = 240 km.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Laterite nickel deposits form by:
options: Tropical weathering of ultramafic rocks concentrating Ni
questions.solution: Laterite: tropical weathering enriches Ni and Co.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Re-Os dating:
options: ¹⁸⁷Re → ¹⁸⁷Os (β⁻). T½ = 41.6 Ga. For sulfides, black shale, oil. Mantle vs crust signature
questions.solution: Re-Os: direct dating of ore minerals. Orogenic gold, VMS. Mantle: ¹⁸⁷Os/¹⁸⁸Os ~0.13.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fault zone architecture:
questions.solution: Fault core: ≤10% of zone width. Damage zone: increased fracture density. Permeability barrier/conduit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 50000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 50000×30 = 1500000 m³ = 1.5 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The colour of a mineral's powder, seen when it is rubbed on a porcelain plate, is its:
questions.solution: Streak is the colour of the fine powder, often more diagnostic than the surface colour.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Remote sensing of mineralogy: ASTER bands for hydrothermal alteration:
options: SWIR bands (5,6,7,8) for clay, carbonate, sericite
questions.solution: ASTER SWIR: clay (band 6), carbonate (band 8), alunite (band 5). OH absorption features.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Batholith:
options: Large intrusive igneous body (>100 km²), discordant
questions.solution: Batholith: massive granitic intrusion. Root of mountain belts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anhydrite-gypsum relationship:
options: Anhydrite (CaSO₄) dehydrates to gypsum (CaSO₄·2H₂O) near surface. Volume increase ~30%
questions.solution: Anhydrite: deeper, hotter. Gypsum: near surface. Gypsification: volume increase, expansion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gravity method — Bouguer anomaly:
options: Observed gravity — latitude correction — free-air — Bouguer slab + terrain. Δg_B = 2πGρh
questions.solution: Bouguer: density variations in crust. Regional (deep) + residual (shallow). Microgravity: cavities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 50 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SAR interferometry (InSAR):
options: Phase difference between two images. Measures cm-scale deformation
questions.solution: InSAR: DEM (topography) and DInSAR (displacement). Atmospheric artifacts. Persistent scatterers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diagenetic zone of sandstone — mesogenesis:
options: Moderate burial (1-4 km). Quartz overgrowths, pressure solution, carbonate cement
questions.solution: Mesogenesis: mechanical compaction → chemical compaction. Primary porosity loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diaphaneity refers to:
questions.solution: Transparent (light passes), translucent (light diffused), opaque (no light).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stratigraphic correlation: marker beds:
options: Volcanic ash (bentonite), marine transgression, iridium anomaly, magnetic reversal
questions.solution: Bentonite: volcanic ash. Key: widespread, thin, distinct. Iridium: K-Pg boundary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Feldspar group belongs to:
questions.solution: Feldspars: orthoclase (K-spar), plagioclase (Na-Ca). Most abundant crustal mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Balanced cross-section requires:
options: Conservation of bed length and area during restoration
questions.solution: Balanced: no gaps or overlaps after restoration. Bed length and area preserved.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ramsay fold classification (1-3):
options: Class 1: dip isogons converge inward. Class 2: parallel (similar). Class 3: converge outward
questions.solution: Ramsay: based on layer thickness and curvature. Class 1A: thickened at hinge. 1B: parallel (concentric).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 20 m of overburden above a 4 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 20/4 = 5 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 20 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 4 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel pattern: braided vs meandering:
options: Braided: high sediment load, variable discharge. Meandering: stable, fine sediment
questions.solution: Braided: high energy, coarse sediment. Meandering: lower energy, fine sediment, cohesive banks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Meandering river:
options: Single channel, sinuous. Cut bank (outer) + point bar (inner). Scroll bars
questions.solution: Meander: sinuosity > 1.5. Meander wavelength: 10-14× channel width. Neck vs chute cutoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 50 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+25+10) = 61%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Conjugate sets of joints intersect at:
options: The intermediate principal stress direction σ₂
questions.solution: Conjugate shear joints: intersection lineation = σ₂. Acute bisector = σ₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bowen's reaction series:
questions.solution: Early crystals: olivine, Ca-plagioclase. Late: quartz, K-feldspar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seepage under dam:
options: Exit gradient must be safe (< critical gradient). Piping failure
questions.solution: Critical gradient i_c = (G_s-1)/(1+e). Piping: backward erosion. Filter design to prevent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about anticlines is correct?
options: The youngest beds lie in the core of an anticline
The oldest beds lie in the core of an anticline
An anticline is a downward fold
questions.solution: Erosion of an upfold exposes the oldest strata at its core; anticlines are classic hydrocarbon traps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic reflection:
options: Subsurface imaging by reflected P-waves. CMP stacking, migration. Resolution: λ/4 (~20m at 50 Hz)
questions.solution: Reflection: petroleum exploration. 2D, 3D seismic. Vibroseis (land), airgun (marine).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flinn diagram: K = (R-1)/(R-1) where R = axial ratios. K>1 indicates:
questions.solution: K>1: elongation dominant (L-tectonite). K<1: flattening dominant (S-tectonite). K=1: plane strain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carbonate grain types: ooids, peloids, bioclasts, intraclasts. Ooids form in:
options: Warm, shallow, agitated marine water (high energy)
questions.solution: Ooids: spherical, concentric layers. Form in warm, shallow, agitated water. Bahama Banks type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kink bands form in:
options: Strongly foliated rocks under compressive stress parallel to foliation
questions.solution: Kink bands: angular folds in well-foliated rocks. Foliation rotates sharply across kink band.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Volcanogenic Massive Sulfide (VMS):
options: Cu-Zn-Pb ± Au-Ag. Submarine volcanic exhalative. Kuroko (Japan), Noranda, Kidd Creek
questions.solution: VMS: stratiform, lens-shaped. Stringer zone (feeder) + massive ore. Besshi, Cyprus, Kuroko types.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Migmatite represents:
questions.solution: Migamatite: mixed rock (leucosome + melanosome). Represents partial melting at deep crustal levels. Transition to igneous.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 0.7 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×0.7 = 0.7 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tsunamis:
options: Ocean wave from underwater earthquake (M>7, thrust fault). Velocity ~√(gh)
questions.solution: Tsunami: long wavelength (>100 km). Wave height increases in shallow water. Run-up.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hummocky cross-stratification (HCS):
options: Formed by storm waves below fair-weather wave base. Undulating sets, low angle
questions.solution: HCS: diagnostic of storm-dominated shelves. Hummocks: 3D cross-sets. Swales: concave down.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trilobite eyes: compound eyes made of:
questions.solution: Trilobite eyes: calcite lenses. Holochroal (many lenses) or schizochroal (fewer, larger lenses).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock body that can store and transmit usable amounts of groundwater is a(n):
questions.solution: An aquifer is porous and permeable enough to yield water to wells.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coal seam gas content measurement:
options: Desorption canister method. Measure gas released over time, extrapolate to total gas
questions.solution: Canister: sealed coal sample, measure desorbed gas. Extrapolate using √t or diffusion model.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porphyry copper — alteration zones:
options: Potassic (center) → phyllic → argillic → propylitic (outer). Sericite, chlorite, clay
questions.solution: Porphyry: Cu-Mo. Low grade (0.3-1% Cu). Large tonnage. Stockwork veins. Chile, USA, Indonesia.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pleochroism in biotite:
options: Strong — dark brown to pale yellow (brown mineral)
questions.solution: Biotite: strong pleochroism. One cleavage direction. 'Bird's eye' extinction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biostratigraphic zone: range zone defined by:
options: First and last appearance of index fossil
questions.solution: Range zone: FAD to LAD of index fossil. Assemblage zone: characteristic assemblage.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oxide copper resources amenable to:
questions.solution: Oxide Cu: soluble in sulfuric acid. Heap leach or in-situ.
